---
title: "Slipway"
subtitle: "Everything your deck can do — right in your browser"
author: "Your Name"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: assets/theme.scss
    navigation-mode: linear
    controls-layout: bottom-right
    center: false
    slide-number: true
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-color: "#B8D6EE"
bibliography: assets/references.bib
---

# Getting started

## The editor

**Left pane** — edit Markdown + YAML frontmatter here.

**⌘R** (or the **Render** button) — compile and preview the deck.

**⌘S** — save. Autosave also runs every 2 seconds.

::: notes
Speaker notes go here — visible in the presenter view (press **S** while
presenting on a desktop browser). This slide is inside the "Getting
started" section, which is created by the `#` heading above it.
:::

## Files & projects

| Action | How |
|---|---|
| Open a file | Click it in the **Files** panel |
| Rename / delete | Action buttons on each row |
| Add an image | Drag onto the **Files** panel |
| Insert + link an image | **Insert image…** toolbar button |
| Import a project | **Import zip** — replaces the current project |
| Export a project | **Export zip** — downloads a `.zip` |

# Text & structure

## Inline formatting

**Bold**, _italic_, ~~strikethrough~~, `inline code`.

> Blockquote — handy for pull quotes or key takeaways.

[Links](https://pandoc.org) work inline. Footnotes
too.^[Footnotes render at the bottom of the slide.]

Span classes from `assets/theme.scss`:
[highlighted sky]{.hlg} · [highlighted sand]{.sand}

## Headings inside a slide

Headings below `##` become **in-slide subheadings**, not new slides.

### This is an H3

Content under the H3 stays on the same slide.

#### H4 and below work too

Use them sparingly — keep slides readable at a distance.

## Tables

| Feature | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Pandoc citeproc | ✓ | `.bib` via YAML frontmatter |
| KaTeX math | ✓ | works offline |
| SCSS themes | ✓ | compiled by Dart Sass |
| `{{< include >}}` | ✓ | pre-processed before pandoc |
| Code execution | — | out of scope by design |

# Lists & fragments

## Incremental list

Wrap any list in `::: {.incremental}` to reveal items one by one:

::: {.incremental}
- First item — visible immediately
- Second item — press **→** to reveal
- Third item — and so on
:::

Works with ordered lists too.

## Fragments on any element

The `.fragment` class works on any fenced div, not just lists:

::: {.fragment}
A whole paragraph that fades in.
:::

::: {.fragment .highlight-red}
Text that highlights red.
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-out}
Text that fades out.
:::

## Fragment order

Control reveal order with `fragment-index`:

::: {.fragment fragment-index=2}
I appear **second**.
:::

::: {.fragment fragment-index=1}
I appear **first** — even though I'm written second.
:::

# Layouts

## Two columns

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="55%"}
**Left (55 %)**

Any content works here: text, code, images, lists.
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
**Right (45 %)**

Column widths must sum to 100 %.

- Lists
- Work fine
- Here too
:::

::::

## Three columns

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="33%"}
**One-third**

Short blurb.
:::

::: {.column width="34%"}
**One-third**

Another blurb.
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
**One-third**

Third blurb.
:::

::::

# Code & math

## Code blocks {.smaller}

Syntax highlighting for dozens of languages, no execution:

```python
def fibonacci(n: int) -> list[int]:
    """Yield the first n Fibonacci numbers."""
    a, b = 0, 1
    result = []
    for _ in range(n):
        result.append(a)
        a, b = b, a + b
    return result
```

Use `{.smaller}` on the slide heading to shrink the font.

## Math

Rendered by KaTeX — works fully offline.

Inline: $f(x) = \frac{1}{\sigma\sqrt{2\pi}}\,e^{-\frac{(x-\mu)^2}{2\sigma^2}}$

Display:

$$
\hat{\boldsymbol{\beta}} =
  \bigl(\mathbf{X}^\top \mathbf{X}\bigr)^{-1}\mathbf{X}^\top \mathbf{y}
$$

# Slide attributes

## Centred slide {.center}

This slide has `{.center}` on the heading, so content is vertically
centred in the frame.

Useful for chapter-break slides or key quotes.

## Smaller font {.smaller}

`{.smaller}` on the heading reduces the font size — good for slides with
more content than usual. Combine with trimming the copy if it still feels
dense: slides are not documents.

Attributes you can put on any `##` heading:

| Attribute | Effect |
|---|---|
| `{.center}` | Vertically centre content |
| `{.smaller}` | Reduce font size |
| `{.incremental}` | Animate all lists on this slide |

## Coloured background {background-color="#F0D5B5"}

The heading carries `{background-color="#F0D5B5"}` (sand).

Any CSS colour works. Images too:

```markdown
## Photo slide {background-image="assets/photo.jpg"}
## Darker slide {background-color="#1c2428"}
```

## {{< include >}} shortcode

{{< include _snippet.md >}}

The content above is pulled from `_snippet.qmd` at render time — Slipway
preprocesses `{{< include >}}` before handing off to Pandoc.

Useful for shared slide fragments, recurring disclaimers, or boilerplate.

# Citations & references

## Citations

Declare a `.bib` file in the YAML frontmatter, then cite inline:

```markdown
bibliography: assets/references.bib
```

Pandoc [@pandoc] converts Markdown to reveal.js HTML. The presentation
framework is reveal.js [@revealjs].

A bibliography slide is appended automatically by the `#refs` div.

## Images

Drop files onto the **Files** panel to add them by original filename, then
reference by path:

```markdown
![A caption](assets/photo.jpg)
![Sized to 70 %](assets/photo.jpg){width=70%}
```

Or use **Insert image…** — saves to `assets/` with a timestamp filename
and inserts `![](…)` at the cursor. Images are inlined as data URIs on
render, so they work offline and survive export.

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
